description: Contracts-as-data: real boundaries in SpeechAngel where a change on one side must be mirrored on the other. The classifier (scripts/workflow/classify.mjs) matches changed paths against `patterns` (JS regex, tested against each path) and surfaces the followUps. Advisory, not a hard gate. Author entries from boundaries you ACTUALLY trip (meta Wave 6).

contracts.title: Always-on microphone foreground service ↔ AndroidManifest service-type sync
contracts.description: A microphone foreground service must declare foregroundServiceType="microphone" AND the FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MICROPHONE permission in its AndroidManifest. Touching a service or the manifest means both sides must stay in sync (research/04_build_and_reuse_plan.md §1 CAPTURE layer).

contracts.title: New dependency ↔ version-catalog entry (no hardcoded coordinates, no dynamic versions)
contracts.description: Every dependency goes through gradle/libs.versions.toml — never a hardcoded "group:artifact:version" in a module build.gradle.kts, and never a dynamic version (+, latest.release, SNAPSHOT). Touching a build file or the catalog means re-running the reproducibility gates.

followUps.label: Verify all deps go through the catalog
Verify no dynamic versions in the catalog

contracts.title: Incident note ↔ knowledge-index closure
contracts.description: Adding or editing an incident under docs/errors/ means the learning loop must be closed: required sections filled, prevention links real, index regenerated. Otherwise knowledge:check blocks the commit.

contracts.title: Eval code or accuracy claims ↔ EVAL measurement discipline
contracts.description: Any change to core:eval, core:matching, core:enrollment, core:dsp, scripts/eval, or accuracy-claim docs must comply with EVAL-002 (held-out thresholds), EVAL-003 (pre-registration/banked-vs-exploratory), and EVAL-005 (replication claim). The verify-sota-measurement.mjs guardrail enforces citation of these in testing docs.

contracts.title: SOTA domain band doc ↔ measurement baseline consistency
contracts.description: The SOTA domain bands doc (docs/product/2026-07-08_sota-domain-bands.md) defines the SOTA=1000 ceiling across 15 domains. Any change that would alter a band threshold or a claimed baseline should be validated against the measurement scripts listed in the Validation scripts index. The followUp runs the full guardrail suite to catch any downstream eval discipline violations the edit may trigger.

followUps.label: Validate SOTA band consistency + run full guardrail suite

contracts.title: QbeEncoder interface ↔ trained model ↔ eval results sync
contracts.description: The QbeEncoder seam (core:matching/QbeEncoder.kt) defines the interface for learned few-shot embeddings. A change to the encoder interface, the model training pipeline, or the eval results that reference QbE accuracy must stay consistent: the 2×2 decomposition (representation × matcher) must hold, the model size constraint (≤2MB on-device) must be satisfied, and the eval must reproduce the baseline before claiming a delta.

contracts.title: Dtw / TemplateMatcher changes ↔ 2×2 decomposition holds
contracts.description: The CP-1 ceiling spike proved that representation and matcher are separate levers: WavLM+DTW ties MFCC-DTW, MFCC+pooling drops to 39.3%. Any change to the DTW implementation, the TemplateMatcher, or the embedding pooler must verify that the 2×2 decomposition still isolates the correct lever — i.e., representation quality and matcher quality must be independently testable, not confounded.

followUps.label: Verify 2×2 decomposition still holds (representation × matcher cross-test)
followUps.command: python3 scripts/eval/ssl_frontend_spike/matcher2x2.py F01 F03 F04 FC01 FC02 FC03 && echo 'Verify: WavLM+DTW ≈ MFCC-DTW (tie), and MFCC+pooling < MFCC-DTW (confound check)'
